summary: Joel briefly describes his experience implementing a picture editor feature using a third-party library (LEADTOOLS). He notes the actual coding took about four hours, but the surrounding work of figuring out deployment dependencies and reading documentation consumed nearly as much additional time. The post is a micro-update reflecting the realities of integrating third-party components into shipping software.
key_points: Implementing a feature-rich picture editor (crop, resize, rotate, undo, save) took only four hours using a third-party library
Figuring out which OCX/DLL files to ship and integrating them into the installer took an equally long four hours
The hidden costs of third-party component integration—deployment, documentation, packaging—rival the actual development time
money_quotes: Not bad: about four hours of work to implement a picture editor with crop, resize, rotate, undo, and save.
It did take about four hours yesterday to figure out which OCX/DLL files I need to ship and make sure that SETUP included them.
key_insight: The actual coding of a feature is often only half the work; packaging, deployment, and dependency management consume just as much time.
